justification: All three input sources (Bird, MJ, Pippen) are represented. Both domain rules preserved with exact values: $0.01 floor labeled as a domain invariant and quantity >= 10 boundary exact. All 3 ACs present with correct values ($4.50, $5.00, $0.01 floor). Pippen's operational notes explicitly included: JSDoc requirement marked as a must-do (not optional), high-traffic context (500 req/sec), unit tests only. No invented information.

observations.text: All three agent sources synthesized into a coherent brief with clear sections
All 3 ACs present with exact given/when/then values including AC3 (floor case)
JSDoc requirement is an explicit architecture decision, not optional — includes rationale about on-call engineers
$0.01 floor clearly labeled as a domain rule with testable assertion
Quantity boundary preserved exactly as >= 10 with boundary test cases at 9/10
Terminology alignment section maps domain terms to technical implementations
Low-confidence flag about floating-point precision is a useful addition without being invented information
